name: Vladimir Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the historic colour of the Vladimir-Suzdal principality
the gold lion of the regional arms

description: A horizontal bicolour of red over a narrow blue stripe at the foot, with the gold crowned lion of Vladimir holding a silver long cross centred on the red field.
history: The flag of Vladimir Oblast was adopted on 23 May 1999. The lion is one of the oldest dynastic emblems in Russian heraldry, used by the Vladimir-Suzdal principality from at least the 12th century.

features.symbolism: a broad red field above a narrow blue stripe at the foot, recalling the colours of the historic Vladimir-Suzdal arms.
a standing gold lion, crowned, holding a silver long cross, the historic arms of Vladimir-Suzdal dating to the 12th century.
